extractions.problem: Multicellular biological systems require models integrating cell signalling, population behaviour, and the extracellular environment, but existing tools do not bridge intracellular Boolean signalling with agent-based multicellular modelling.
extractions.method: Combined Boolean modelling (MaBoSS) for intracellular signalling with agent-based modelling (PhysiCell) using OpenMP parallelization and BioFVM diffusion solver, simulating off-lattice cells as soft spheres in 2D/3D.
extractions.finding: PhysiBoSS bridges single-cell genotype to emergent multicellular behaviour; demonstrated heterogeneous cell fate decisions under TNF treatment and showed spatial resource competition (oxygen) critically influences population dynamics.
extractions.dataset: Simulated tumour cell populations with TNF-induced cell fate Boolean model (Calzone et al. 2010)
extractions.metric: Cell fate ratios (survival/apoptosis/NonACD), population dynamics over time
extractions.result: Scales to 10^5–10^6 cells; hours of compute for thousands of cells over several simulated days

extractions.problem: The nature of cellular constraints driving metabolic rewiring in compartmentalised eukaryal cells, particularly the Crabtree effect and overflow metabolism in yeast, remains undetermined.
extractions.method: Comprehensive proteome-constrained genome-scale metabolic model (pcYeast) of S. cerevisiae coupling metabolic fluxes to enzyme synthesis/degradation with compartment-specific protein pool constraints and growth rate maximisation; validated with chemostat and batch experiments.
extractions.finding: Under glucose limitation a mitochondrial protein constraint triggers ethanol formation (Crabtree effect); under sugar excess a cytosolic volume constraint dictates overflow metabolism. Active constraints are condition-dependent.
extractions.dataset: S. cerevisiae CEN.PK 113-7D, chemostats at D=0.2–0.34 h⁻¹, ~3000 proteins quantified by label-free proteomics
extractions.metric: Growth rate (h⁻¹), metabolic fluxes (mmol/gDW/h), proteome mass fractions
extractions.result: Correctly predicted mCherry overexpression growth rate reduction; identified distinct mitochondrial vs cytosolic constraints at critical dilution rate

extractions.problem: Whether a head-restrained mouse virtual reality system permitting horizontal rotations can reproduce the 2D spatial firing patterns of place, grid, and head-direction cells characteristic of real open arenas.
extractions.method: Novel VR system with air-supported Styrofoam ball, 4 LCD screens + 2 projectors, and tetrode electrophysiology from hippocampal CA1 and entorhinal cortex; compared spatial firing properties between VR and matched real environment.
extractions.finding: Place and grid cells fired with ~1.4× broader spatial tuning in VR; place cells were more directional in VR; theta-speed relationship was reduced; but firing rate–speed modulation and theta phase precession were similar between VR and real.
extractions.dataset: 231 CA1 cells from 7 mice (179 place cells in real, 185 in VR, 154 in both); 11 mice total
extractions.metric: Spatial information (bits/spike), spatial tuning width, directional selectivity, theta frequency, phase precession slope
extractions.result: Spatial scale ~1.4× larger in VR vs real; fading-beacon task learned within 2–3 weeks (p<0.001, F(3,30)=39.03)

extractions.problem: Systematic gene knockout experiments are prohibitively expensive; a computational virtual KO tool is needed to infer gene function from single-cell RNA-seq data without requiring KO samples.
extractions.method: scTenifoldKnk constructs a gene regulatory network from wild-type scRNA-seq via principal component regression and tensor decomposition, virtually deletes a target gene, then uses manifold alignment to identify differentially regulated genes.
extractions.finding: Virtual KO analysis recapitulates main findings of three real in vivo KO experiments (Nkx2-1, Klf5, Foxl1) and recovers expected gene functions in relevant cell types using only wild-type data.
extractions.dataset: Mouse lung epithelial cells (2,312 WT cells, Nkx2-1 KO), mouse intestinal cells (Klf5, Foxl1 KO studies)
extractions.metric: Gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA), differentially regulated (DR) gene overlap
extractions.baseline: Real in vivo KO experiments with scRNA-seq + bulk RNA-seq + ChIP-seq + immunostaining
extractions.result: Overall consistency across 3 KO validations; DR genes enriched for expected biological functions (AT1/AT2 markers, gastrointestinal markers)

extractions.problem: Need integrative tools to identify individual cells making transitions between states, infer transition-driving genes, and uncover the role of intermediate cell states (ICS) in epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition.
extractions.method: QuanTC: unsupervised learning combining consensus clustering, NMF soft clustering, and Cell Plasticity Index (CPI) with multiscale agent-based stochastic modelling of a core EMT gene regulatory network.
extractions.finding: ICS-mediated transition trajectories attenuate noise and enhance signal adaptation in EMT; cancer EMT strengthens adaptation while embryogenesis EMT favours effective population transition toward mesenchymal states.
extractions.dataset: 12 published single-cell EMT datasets in cancer and embryogenesis (including SCC, A549 lung cancer)
extractions.metric: Cell Plasticity Index (CPI), Spearman's rank correlation (threshold 0.64), transition efficiency
extractions.baseline: Monocle 3 pseudotime inference, direct clustering methods
extractions.result: QuanTC distinguishes transition cells with higher resolution than Monocle 3; reveals trade-off between adaptation, noise attenuation, and transition efficiency

extractions.problem: Computational tools for genome design are scarce; algorithms are needed to enable design-simulate-test cycles for genome minimisation using whole-cell models.
extractions.method: Two algorithms—Minesweeper (divide-and-conquer) and GAMA (biased genetic algorithm)—iteratively delete genes from the M. genitalium whole-cell model, simulating cell division as the functional assay.
extractions.finding: Discovered in silico minimal genomes smaller than JCVI-Syn3.0; identified 10 low essential genes and evidence for at least two distinct M. genitalium in silico minimal genomes.
extractions.dataset: M. genitalium whole-cell model (401 genes, 359 protein-coding)
extractions.metric: Genome size (gene count), cell division (binary pass/fail), replicate consistency (10 replicates)
extractions.baseline: JCVI-Syn3.0 (473 genes), previous reduced Mycoplasma predictions (413 genes)
extractions.result: Minesweeper_256 (36% reduction), GAMA_237 (41% reduction); 58,071 total genomes simulated

extractions.problem: Whole-cell modelling lacks community standards and software tools, hindering construction and sharing of comprehensive computational cell models.
extractions.method: Organised the 2015 Whole-Cell Modeling Summer School to evaluate needs by recoding the M. genitalium whole-cell model in SBML; conducted community discussions to identify gaps.
extractions.finding: Identified critical needs: high-performance parallel simulator supporting SBML Multistate/Spatial packages, model design software, data curation tools, and extended SBGN for hybrid diagrams.
extractions.dataset: M. genitalium whole-cell model (Karr et al. 2012)
extractions.baseline: Existing SBML standards and Karr et al. whole-cell model implementation

extractions.problem: The mechanisms by which type IV pili govern formation and dynamics of Neisseria gonorrhoeae microcolonies—from single-cell motility to colony self-assembly—are poorly understood.
extractions.method: Agent-based model with explicit pili dynamics, force generation, and pili-pili interactions; Langevin-type equations for cell motion driven by pili retraction forces on surfaces and between cells.
extractions.finding: Pili-generated forces are central to colony formation; model reproduces bimodal velocity distributions, persistent motion, and colony merging dynamics consistent with experimental observations.
extractions.dataset: N. gonorrhoeae microcolonies (simulated single cells to multi-colony aggregates)
extractions.metric: Cell velocity distribution, velocity autocorrelation function (double-exponential fit), colony merging dynamics
extractions.baseline: Available experimental observations of N. gonorrhoeae surface motility and colony formation
extractions.result: Single-pilus peak velocity ~2 μm/s; short correlation time τ₁~0.1s, long persistence τ₂~seconds; agreement with experiments

extractions.problem: Refined biophysical models of C. elegans neuron dynamics are needed to describe electrophysiological processes at cellular and network scales, given the fully mapped nervous system.
extractions.method: Hodgkin-Huxley-based models of 15 ion channel types fitted to C. elegans and homologous organism data, combined into whole-cell models of AWCon (chemosensory) and RMD (motor) neurons with intracellular calcium dynamics.
extractions.finding: Model reproduces regenerative responses of both neurons; RMD exhibits heterogeneous dynamics including bistable regimes and sustained oscillations, critically regulated by CCA-1 T-type calcium and leakage currents.
extractions.dataset: C. elegans neurons AWCon and RMD (electrophysiology data from C. elegans and homologous organisms)
extractions.metric: Peak/steady-state current amplitudes, activation/inactivation time constants (least-squares nonlinear fitting)
extractions.baseline: Experimental voltage-clamp recordings from C. elegans and heterologous expression systems
extractions.result: Reproduced regenerative responses; identified bistable and oscillatory regimes in RMD via CCA-1 and leak current modulation

extractions.problem: Rules-based models are needed to explain non-intuitive emergent behaviour of heterogeneous cell populations in dynamic microenvironments, encouraging computational-experimental collaboration.
extractions.method: ARCADE: multi-scale agent-based model in Java/MASON with interface-based extensible framework incorporating intracellular metabolism (Michaelis-Menten) and signalling (PLCγ) modules for cell agents in hexagonal grid environments.
extractions.finding: Emergent behaviour differs between colony and tissue contexts; parameter variation produces linear, non-linear, and multimodal effects on co-culture competition; cell and population heterogeneity variably impact outcomes.
extractions.dataset: Simulated tissue cell populations (healthy, cancerous, cancer stem cell subtypes)
extractions.metric: Growth dynamics, population cell counts, spatial distribution over 14-day simulations
extractions.baseline: Literature-derived parameters with no additional fitting
extractions.result: 14-day simulation runs in 5–10 min CPU; biologically realistic growth dynamics without parameter fitting
